notes: Human-ratified labels (2026-07-06). Ground truth established by source-inspection of the reference fetch server and a live stdio tools/list capture (server 1.28.1, protocol 2025-06-18) on 2026-07-06. The tool advertises NO MCP annotations, and it reaches an arbitrary caller-supplied URL on the open internet, so the absent openWorldHint is an honest gap a `reach-absent` nudge should surface. This is a true-positive recall check.

provenance.source: github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers, src/fetch/src/mcp_server_fetch/server.py — the `fetch` tool retrieves an arbitrary `url` (AnyUrl) from the internet and returns its contents; no annotations are declared
provenance.notes: Read-only with respect to the caller's state, but genuinely open-world: it can reach any host the caller names. The `url` parameter is destination-shaped, so the v0 engine sees the reach from the schema.
